reference_model.name: ISTQB test harness glossary
reference_model.applied_as: Treat the dev harness as the driver, fixtures, test data, and oracle layer around project changes.

reference_model.name: pytest fixtures
reference_model.applied_as: Make setup explicit, modular, scoped, and reusable instead of hidden in ad-hoc scripts.

reference_model.name: Google small/medium/large tests
reference_model.applied_as: Classify checks by scope, not by vague unit/integration labels only.

reference_model.name: OpenAI evals guide
reference_model.applied_as: Define expected behavior before implementation, then run deterministic and model-facing evals.

reference_model.name: GitHub Actions CI
reference_model.applied_as: Keep the same checks runnable locally and in CI/PR review.

reference_model.name: Playwright isolation
reference_model.applied_as: Use clean contexts/profiles for browser and multi-user verification.

tiers.smoke: Fast contract checks that should run while coding.
tiers.full: Pre-push checks for broad regressions and formatting issues.

repo-entry.summary: Repository-wide docs, harness maps, and developer workflow entrypoints.
eval_contract: context packs render for touched areas
harness manifests point only to existing source paths
repo-wide docs lint passes
risk_flags: stale context pack points agents at the wrong layer
docs drift makes future agents over-read the repo

goal-driven.summary: Goal-driven agent loop, decision parsing, completion, achievement, and verification logic.
eval_contract: decision schema changes parse into ActionDecision without dropping safety fields
WAIT completion requires evidence, not model assertion alone
goal achievement rejects premature completion when evidence is missing
risk_flags: new heuristic overrides OpenClaw/raw DOM evidence
single-screen completion shortcut masks multi-step business goals

multi-user-interaction.summary: Multi-user interaction skill, participant registry, blackboard events, and event-driven scheduler.
eval_contract: participant_plan.required=false preserves single-user path
participant_plan.required=true boots isolated participant sessions
blackboard events wake only participants whose wake conditions match
multi-user completion uses cross-participant evidence
risk_flags: round-robin fallback accidentally reintroduced
browser profile isolation weakened
credential values leak across participant prompts

openclaw-dispatch.summary: Embedded OpenClaw runtime, MCP local dispatch, profile/session routing, screenshots, and browser actions.
eval_contract: session_id routes to the expected browser target
profile_name is preserved across browser actions
dispatch responses preserve action success/effective evidence
risk_flags: profile/session state leaks across tests
dispatch wrapper hides browser errors

benchmark-harness.summary: Benchmark runner, suite contracts, graders, KPI summary, and reproducible artifacts.
eval_contract: suite JSON validates before execution
summary/results artifacts remain machine-readable
graders fail closed when expected evidence is missing
baseline/candidate comparison reports gate status and deltas before performance claims
risk_flags: benchmark output schema drift
grader accepts weak evidence
comparison artifact is cited without matching baseline and candidate paths

smoke: PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m py_compile scripts/run_goal_benchmark.py scripts/compare_benchmark_runs.py gaia/harness/runner.py gaia/harness/graders/expected_signals.py gaia/harness/graders/membership.py

presentation-prep.summary: Presentation notes, demo evidence, KPI talking points, caveats, and artifact-backed update log.
eval_contract: presentation claims cite artifact paths or source docs
performance claims include gate status, baseline, candidate, and caveat
notes preserve next-update items instead of scattering presentation state across chat
risk_flags: numbers copied without artifact provenance
demo story overstates small benchmark evidence
temporary artifacts are garbage-collected before notes capture the usable result

runtime-entrypoints.summary: CLI, terminal, chat hub, GUI worker, auth, and session cleanup entrypoints.
eval_contract: entrypoints route to the same goal-driven runtime contract
cleanup hooks close temporary participant sessions
user-facing help text matches accepted command syntax
risk_flags: terminal/chat/gui diverge in runtime behavior
cleanup skipped on exceptions

smoke: PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m py_compile gaia/cli.py gaia/terminal.py gaia/chat_hub.py gaia/auth.py gaia/src/gui/goal_worker.py

cleanup-gc.summary: Garbage collection, stale artifacts, dead fallback removal, and repo weight control.
eval_contract: source-of-truth paths are not deleted
generated caches/artifacts remain outside committed source
cleanup rules are documented before destructive cleanup
risk_flags: destructive cleanup removes user WIP
large generated directories re-enter git status

development-harness.summary: Development harness lane metadata, RevFactory adaptation, lane detection, linting, and team/check orchestration.
eval_contract: development-harness lane can be planned and detected directly
lane detection maps changed paths to the correct context/check lane
lint rejects drift between context manifest and development harness manifest
RevFactory phase/team metadata stays machine-readable
risk_flags: context area exists but no matching development lane can be selected
lane detection misses touched files and agents run the wrong checks
team_pattern or recommended_agents drift from the documented architecture

revfactory_mapping.adaptation: Codex-native development harness: context packs and scripts replace .claude agents/skills, while preserving audit, team architecture, orchestration, validation, and evolution phases.

phases.name: agent_definition
phases.local_mechanism: AGENT_HARNESS_PLAYBOOK roles and Codex subagent roles when explicitly requested

phases.name: skill_definition
phases.local_mechanism: context packs, existing Codex skills, and lane-specific check commands

phases.name: validation
phases.local_mechanism: lint_harness_docs, lane tests, full unit suite, git diff --check

patterns.pipeline: Planner -> Developer -> Verifier -> Cleanup for dependent changes.
patterns.fan-out/fan-in: Parallel independent investigation, then one synthesis pass.
patterns.expert-pool: Invoke only the specialist lane needed by the changed subsystem.
patterns.producer-reviewer: Developer produces a patch; Verifier independently challenges evidence.
patterns.supervisor: Main Codex agent tracks lane plan, risk flags, commands, and final report.
patterns.hierarchical-delegation: Use only for large cross-lane changes; keep depth to two levels.

planner.role: Scope the problem, non-goals, affected lane, eval contract, and risk flags.
planner.codex_mapping: local reasoning; spawn planner only when user explicitly asks for subagents.

developer.role: Make the minimal code/docs change and preserve unrelated work.
developer.codex_mapping: main agent or executor subagent for disjoint write scopes when explicitly delegated.

verifier.role: Independently rerun lane checks and challenge completion evidence.
verifier.codex_mapping: verifier/code-reviewer subagent only when explicitly requested; otherwise run deterministic checks locally.

cleanup.role: Remove stale artifacts, duplicate fallback, and cache output without touching user WIP.

data_protocol: Planner brief is the input contract for Developer.
Developer output includes changed files, commands run, skipped checks, and residual risks.
Verifier consumes artifacts and commands before trusting Developer interpretation.
Cleanup records generated artifacts/caches removed and source-of-truth paths preserved.
